plan.title: audit-02: identify and remove repo-scale persistence and refresh work hidden behind narrow update paths, then land targeted fixes in priority order
plan.goal: audit-02: identify and remove repo-scale persistence and refresh work hidden behind narrow update paths, then land targeted fixes in priority order

node.title: Instrument and rank hidden repo-scale persistence work
node.summary: Audited narrow refresh and mutation paths for hidden repo-scale work. The highest-priority hotspots are: sqlite index persistence still spending most time in global side-data writes rather than file-state upserts; outcome and episodic snapshot saves reloading whole event logs inside hot transactions; projection delta application issuing thousands of co-change upserts plus a global prune on tiny refreshes; targeted index persistence still rewriting derived-edge and finalize state repo-wide. The audit also identified full-snapshot history replacement and full-table inference reconciliation as broader patterns that should stay in scope while fixing the hot paths.

acceptance.label: The dominant repo-scale costs on narrow refresh and mutation paths are identified with concrete code anchors and runtime evidence.

node.title: Remove full-log snapshot reloads from auxiliary persistence
node.summary: Investigating how outcome and episodic snapshot saves compute append-only deltas today so the hot write path can stop reloading whole event logs inside the transaction.

acceptance.label: Outcome and episodic persistence stop reconstructing whole snapshots inside hot write transactions.

node.title: Remove full-log snapshot reloads from auxiliary persistence
node.summary: Removed the hot-path full-log reloads from auxiliary snapshot persistence by introducing revision-guarded outcome and episodic snapshot caches in SqliteStore, reusing them during snapshot saves, and falling back to transaction-local reloads only when revisions drift. Added stale-cache regressions for auxiliary outcome and episodic snapshot saves and kept outcome cache normalization aligned with the existing hot-patch compaction behavior.

acceptance.label: Outcome and episodic persistence stop reconstructing whole snapshots inside hot write transactions.

node.title: Make projection maintenance proportional to changed scope
node.summary: Investigating co-change delta persistence and prune scope so tiny refresh batches stop paying repo-wide projection maintenance costs.

acceptance.label: Co-change and validation projection persistence no longer does repo-wide maintenance for tiny deltas without justification.

node.title: Make projection maintenance proportional to changed scope
node.summary: Made co-change projection maintenance proportional to the current batch by aggregating duplicate deltas before SQLite writes and pruning only the touched source lineages instead of running a repo-wide rank-and-delete on every delta batch. Added a regression that documents untouched overflow rows now stay in place until explicit maintenance, while touched sources still trim to the top-K policy.

acceptance.label: Co-change and validation projection persistence no longer does repo-wide maintenance for tiny deltas without justification.

node.title: Eliminate global graph rewrites from targeted index persistence
node.summary: Investigating how targeted index persistence can rewrite only affected derived edges and root/package finalize state instead of rewriting repo-wide graph state on every small batch.

acceptance.label: Targeted index persistence no longer rewrites repo-wide derived-edge or finalize state unless the change genuinely requires it.

node.title: Validate persistence hot-path improvements with runtime evidence
node.summary: Consolidating store-test coverage, release-daemon verification, and fresh runtime samples for the persistence hot-path changes. Post-restart health is green; a fresh post-restart targeted refresh sample is still the main missing live datapoint.

acceptance.label: Runtime logs and focused regressions show materially lower narrow-scope persist and refresh costs after the fixes.

node.title: Eliminate global graph rewrites from targeted index persistence
node.summary: Eliminated repo-wide derived-edge rewrites from targeted index persistence by collecting stale plus current node ids for changed files and rewriting only derived edges incident to those nodes. Added store coverage proving unrelated derived edges survive a touched-node rewrite, and tightened the SQLite persist log so rewritten_derived_edge_count now reports the actual touched-scope rewrite volume instead of global graph size.

acceptance.label: Targeted index persistence no longer rewrites repo-wide derived-edge or finalize state unless the change genuinely requires it.

node.title: Validate persistence hot-path improvements with runtime evidence
node.summary: Validated the persistence hot-path work with package tests, required prism-mcp contract tests, a release rebuild, and a live post-restart daemon sample. Fresh one-file FsWatch refreshes now log rewritten_derived_edge_count=48, save_outcomes_ms=4-5, persist_projection_ms=2, and persist_ms=302-303, confirming the auxiliary snapshot reload and projection work no longer dominate tiny refresh batches; the derived-edge rewrite counter now reflects the actual touched-node rewrite scope.

acceptance.label: Runtime logs and focused regressions show materially lower narrow-scope persist and refresh costs after the fixes.

node.title: Instrument and rank hidden repo-scale persistence work
node.summary: Audited narrow refresh and mutation paths for hidden repo-scale work. The highest-priority hotspots are: sqlite index persistence still spending most time in global side-data writes rather than file-state upserts; outcome and episodic snapshot saves reloading whole event logs inside hot transactions; projection delta application issuing thousands of co-change upserts plus a global prune on tiny refreshes; targeted index persistence still rewriting derived-edge and finalize state repo-wide. The audit also identified full-snapshot history replacement and full-table inference reconciliation as broader patterns that should stay in scope while fixing the hot paths.

acceptance.label: The dominant repo-scale costs on narrow refresh and mutation paths are identified with concrete code anchors and runtime evidence.
